name: claude-for-codex
description: Use Claude Code from Codex for read-only review, adversarial review, native SDK subagent teams, role-pack review teams, advisory mailbox/leases, planning, optional SDK backend, GitHub Actions PR review templates, rescue, ultrareview, and tracked job workflows.

interface.shortDescription: Claude Code reviews, planning, and stop-gate workflows for Codex.
interface.longDescription: Claude for Codex connects Codex to the local Claude Code CLI for independent read-only code review, adversarial design critique, structured adversarial verdicts, implementation planning, read-only rescue diagnosis, multi-role review fan-out, native SDK subagent review teams, background job tracking, lifecycle hooks, capability diagnostics, optional semantic context, sanitized review reports, release checks, and an opt-in Stop hook review gate. It is designed for multi-model collaboration: Codex implements and reconciles, while Claude provides a separate reviewer or planner perspective. Claude invocations are constrained to read-only tools for review workflows, native SDK mode is explicit, ultrareview requires cost consent, and the Stop gate fails open on Claude runtime errors unless Claude explicitly returns a blocking verdict.
capabilities: GitHub Actions PR review workflow templates
defaultPrompt: Use Claude to review the current git changes.
Ask Claude to challenge this implementation plan.
Run a multi-role Claude review across correctness, security, tests, release, and adversarial perspectives.
